summary: ts-form-utils ships system reconnaissance and exfiltration code in lib/perf.js
details: The package advertises itself as a TypeScript form utilities library but ships lib/perf.js, which combines os/child_process/https primitives in the canonical host-reconnaissance-and-exfiltration shape: it imports os, http, https, and child_process; calls os.hostname() in multiple locations; runs execSync to gather host data; references curl; and issues HTTPS POST requests with hostname-derived data. This pattern (system info collection + outbound POST to a non-registry endpoint) is unrelated to the package's stated purpose of form utilities and provides no value to a consumer of a TS form helper. Any installer that requires or transitively pulls this module exposes host identifiers and command output to a remote endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
